audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for transfiguration, Elijah/John pattern, difficult healing, trust/untrust, prayer variant, second passion prediction, child/greatness, outsider exorcist, little ones, Gehenna, salt/fire sayings.

verses.restored: He said to them, Amen, I say to you: there are some standing here who will certainly not taste death until they see the reign of the Divine having come in power.
After six days Jesus takes Peter, James, and John and brings them up into a high mountain by themselves. He was transfigured before them.
His garments became radiant, very white, such as no fuller on earth is able to whiten.
Elijah appeared to them with Moses, and they were speaking with Jesus.
Peter answered Jesus, Rabbi, it is beautiful/good for us to be here; let us make three tents, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
For he did not know what to answer, because they were greatly afraid.
A cloud came, overshadowing them, and a voice came from the cloud: This is my beloved Son; listen to him.
Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus alone.
As they came down from the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one what they had seen until the Son of Humanity should rise from the dead.
They held the word, discussing among themselves what rising from the dead meant.
They questioned him, saying, Why do the scribes say Elijah must come first?
He said to them, Elijah indeed comes first and restores all things. Yet how has it been written of the Son of Humanity that he should suffer many things and be treated as nothing?
But I say to you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they wished, as it has been written about him.
Coming to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them and scribes arguing with them.
Immediately, seeing him, the whole crowd was greatly amazed and ran to greet him.
He asked them, What are you arguing about with them?
One from the crowd answered, Teacher, I brought my son to you, having a spirit that makes him unable to speak.
Wherever it seizes him, it throws him down; he foams, grinds his teeth, and becomes rigid. I told your disciples to cast it out, and they were not strong enough.
He answered them, O untrusting generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me.
They brought him to him. Seeing Jesus, the spirit immediately convulsed him, and falling on the ground he rolled about, foaming.
Jesus asked his father, How long has this been happening to him? He said, From childhood.
Often it has thrown him into fire and into waters to destroy him; but if you are able, have compassion on us and help us.
Jesus said to him, If you are able? All things are possible for the one trusting.
Immediately the child's father cried out, I trust; help my untrust.
Jesus, seeing that a crowd was running together, rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, Speechless and deaf spirit, I command you, come out from him and enter him no more.
After crying out and convulsing him greatly, it came out. He became like a dead one, so that many said, He has died.
But Jesus took him by the hand and raised him, and he rose.
When he entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why were we not able to cast it out?
He said to them, This kind is able to come out by nothing except prayer. [Some manuscripts add: and fasting.]
Going out from there, they passed through Galilee, and he did not want anyone to know.
For he was teaching his disciples and saying to them, The Son of Humanity is being handed over into human hands; they will kill him, and when killed, after three days he will rise.
But they did not understand the word and were afraid to ask him.
They came to Capernaum. In the house he asked them, What were you discussing on the way?
But they were silent, for on the way they had argued with one another about who was greatest.
Sitting down, he called the twelve and said to them, If anyone wants to be first, that one shall be last of all and servant of all.
Taking a child, he stood the child in their midst, and taking the child in his arms, he said to them,
Whoever receives one of such children in my name receives me; and whoever receives me receives not me but the One who sent me.
John said to him, Teacher, we saw someone casting out unclean spirits in your name, and we tried to stop him because he was not following us.
But Jesus said, Do not stop him, for no one who does a power in my name will soon be able to speak harmfully of me.
For whoever is not against us is for us.
Whoever gives you a cup of water in name because you are Messiah's, amen, I say to you, will certainly not lose the reward.
Whoever causes one of these little ones trusting in me to stumble, it is better for that one if a large millstone is placed around the neck and that one is thrown into the sea.
If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better to enter life maimed than with two hands to go into Gehenna, into the unquenchable fire.
[Some later manuscripts add: where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.]
If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better to enter life lame than with two feet to be thrown into Gehenna.
[Some later manuscripts add: where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.]
If your eye causes you to stumble, throw it out; it is better to enter the reign of the Divine with one eye than with two eyes to be thrown into Gehenna,
where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.
For everyone will be salted with fire.
Salt is beautiful/good; but if salt becomes unsalty, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.
